rationale: Empirical, run in an isolated /tmp clone pinned to 4dfebbcb (the shared bead worktree was concurrently mutated mid-review by another reviewer's probe — gate.go had `false && len(unresolved)>0` disabling leg 9.5; I restored it, git status clean, and moved all work to the clone). In the clone: `go build ./...` clean; `gofmt -l ./cmd ./internal` empty; `go vet ./internal/panel ./internal/complete` clean; `go test -count=1 ./internal/panel ./internal/complete ./cmd/mindspec` all PASS; `go test ./internal/instruct` PASS incl. TestRun_IdleNoBeads (5x, no z4ps flake this run). HEAD = exactly the 9 files, one commit, no internal/bead, no AppliedRefutations (grep-empty). Break-it probe: (a) 5A+1RC Blocks naming slot; (b) 6/6 all-APPROVE Allows (no false block); (c) unrecognized 'MAYBE'/'LGTM_BUT' Blocks; (c2) lowercase 'approve' Blocks (case-sensitive); (d) sub-threshold RC message keeps '3/6 APPROVE'+'threshold is 5/6' superset; (e) VoteDecision VoteBlocks in lockstep. AC5 fixture flips + preserving updates match the diff exactly.
findings: build/gofmt/vet all clean in isolated clone at 4dfebbcb
go test ./internal/panel ./internal/complete ./cmd/mindspec ./internal/instruct all PASS; TestRun_IdleNoBeads green (no z4ps flake observed across 5 runs)
leg 9.5 correct: fires on UnresolvedVerdicts() (neither APPROVE nor REJECT), placed after leg 9 REJECT, before leg 10 threshold; layered (threshold floor preserved)
byte-superset message verified: sub-threshold RC route retains 'X/N APPROVE' + 'threshold is T/N' leg-10 substrings
VoteDecision twin in lockstep — same RC/unrecognized inputs VoteBlock
scope clean: exactly 9 files, one commit, no internal/bead, no AppliedRefutations (Bead 2's), no 'refut'/SkipPanelEnv in Block message (AC10/HC-7)

PROCESS NOTE (not a bead defect): shared bead worktree gate.go was concurrently mutated by another reviewer during this review (leg 9.5 disabled via 'false &&'); I reverted it and git status is clean. All my results come from an isolated clone, immune to that interference.
Did not observe the full `go test ./...` completion (background run still in flight when verdict was requested); the four in-scope packages plus instruct were exercised directly and passed.
